steps.title: Identify the key contrast
steps.content: The passage states that early quasars formed “without the need for ultraviolet backgrounds or other extreme and implausible environmental conditions that models of the early universe have included.” This reveals that previous models relied on implausible assumptions that the new research shows were unnecessary.

steps.title: Evaluate the choices
steps.content:
A directly follows: if previous models relied on assumptions now shown unnecessary, new models can rest on better-supported assumptions. B contradicts the passage by doubting quasars' existence; C reverses the logical direction; D inverts the cause-consequence relationship.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
